question: The word 'familiarity' means ________.
choices: a good knowledge of a particular subject
a method of testing students on new material
a feeling of excitement about upcoming events
a strong dislike of something unfamiliar

question: Something described as 'thought-provoking' ________.
choices: helps you memorise facts quickly and easily
makes you think seriously about a particular subject
describes something that is entertaining but meaningless
refers to something that is too difficult to understand

question: The word 'silk' refers to ________.
choices: a type of dye used to change the colour of fabric
long, thin strings used in weaving and embroidery
a soft cloth made from something produced by a silkworm
valuable jewels such as diamonds used in decoration

question: The word 'threads' refers to ________.
choices: things used to change the colour of clothes
traditional beliefs passed down through generations
patterns made from coloured dye on cloth
long, thin strings of material

question: The word 'geometric' describes something that ________.
choices: has regular lines or shapes
is made from precious natural materials
represents a traditional cultural practice
refers to embroidery found only in the Arab world

question: If someone is 'in suspense', they feel ________.
choices: completely bored and disinterested
excited and anxious about what will happen next
angry about the result of something
satisfied and relieved after an event

question: Choose the correct preposition: 'She revealed the ending _____ accident; she didn't mean to.'
choices: on
in
by
at

question: The prepositional phrase 'at least' means ________.
choices: more than expected
almost certainly
probably not
not less than

question: The phrase 'under the impression' means ________.
choices: thought / believed
fully convinced and certain
surprised by something new
worried about an outcome

question: The phrase 'in all likelihood' means ________.
choices: it is completely impossible
it is very probable
it is certainly not going to happen
it is difficult to decide

question: The phrase 'in fact' is used to mean ________.
choices: in addition to what was said before
in contrast with what was expected
to tell the truth / in reality
for the reason that something happened

question: The phrase 'on purpose' means ________.
choices: without thinking about it carefully
by accident / unintentionally
because of pressure from others
planned to do / intentionally

question: 'It is possible that the map was drawn by Egyptian sailors in the past.' Choose the correct past modal form.
choices: The map might have been drawn by Egyptian sailors
The map must have been drawn by Egyptian sailors
The map can't have been drawn by Egyptian sailors
The map would be drawn by Egyptian sailors

question: 'The jewellery is solid gold — I am certain it was expensive.' Choose the correct past modal form.
choices: The jewellery can't have been expensive
The jewellery must have been expensive
The jewellery might not have been expensive
The jewellery would have been cheap

question: 'I am certain that these enormous footprints did not belong to any human.' Choose the correct past modal form.
choices: These footprints would have belonged to a human
These footprints might have belonged to a human
These footprints can't have belonged to a human
These footprints must have belonged to a human

question: 'I assume the owner used this ancient tool to navigate across the ocean.' Choose the correct past modal form.
choices: The owner might have used it to navigate
The owner can't have used it to navigate
The owner must have used it to navigate
The owner would have used it to navigate

question: 'I am not sure whether Ali broke the glass or not.' Choose the correct past modal form to rewrite this sentence.
choices: Ali might have broken the glass
Ali must have broken the glass
Ali can't have broken the glass
Ali would have broken the glass

question: Sara seemed very tired today. I think she was revising until late at night. Choose the correct past modal form.
choices: Sara can't have been revising until late
Sara must have been revising until late
Sara might not have been revising
Sara would be revising until late

question: Choose the correct reduced form of: 'As she walked into the room, she smiled warmly.'
choices: Having walked into the room, she smiled warmly
She was walked into the room, smiling warmly
Walking into the room, she smiled warmly
She walked into the room, having smiled warmly

question: We use 'Having + past participle' in a reduced adverbial clause in order to ________.
choices: show that two actions happened at exactly the same time
replace a passive verb form in formal writing
describe a result that follows naturally from an action
emphasise that one action happened before another

question: Choose the correct reduced form of: 'I didn't realise what time it was, so I missed the train.'
choices: Not realising what time it was, I missed the train
Having realised not what time it was, I missed the train
I missed the train, realising what time it was
Without realising what time I was, I missed the train

question: In reduced adverbial clauses, 'despite + verb-ing' is used to replace ________.
choices: because + subject + verb
although + subject + verb
while + subject + verb
as soon as + subject + verb

question: 'The ________ novel is set in medieval Europe and tells the story of a famous knight.' (set in the past)
choices: historic
classic
historical
comical

question: 'The signing of the peace treaty was a ________ occasion that changed the course of the nation.' (important in history)
choices: classical
comical
tasteful
historic

question: The word 'comic' is used ________ a noun to describe things which are meant to be funny.
choices: before
after
instead of
alongside

question: 'She has a ________ way of looking at things that reminds everyone of her innocence.' (positive quality typical of a child)
choices: childish
childlike
classic
comical

question: 'The Internet is an ________ source of information for researchers and students.' (extremely useful)
choices: worthless
insipid
invaluable
subdued

question: Among these adjectives used to describe art and colour, which one is NEGATIVE?
choices: vibrant
distinctive
rich
jarring

question: The word 'plot' refers to ________.
choices: the storyline of a play, film, TV drama, or book
the group of actors taking part in a performance
the final funny line at the end of a joke
the location where a performance takes place

question: The 'punchline' of a joke is ________.
choices: the opening line that introduces the subject of the joke
the final line of the joke that makes people laugh
the part where the comedian describes a funny situation
a rude comment made by a member of the audience

question: A 'sitcom' is ________.
choices: a live comedy performance done on a stage alone
a TV programme that makes fun of public figures
a genre of comedy series broadcast on TV featuring the same actors
a short funny video shared on social media platforms

question: When comedians tell a series of jokes on stage, we say they are ________.
choices: delivering the punchline
heckling the audience
playing the lead role
doing a stand-up routine

question: 'Her performance was truly ________; she was far better than anyone else in the entire cast.' Choose the correct evaluative adjective.
choices: exceptional
unconvincing
offensive
cheesy

question: Read the following excerpt: 'Some people are under the impression that it's because we are in suspense about what is going to happen, so someone actually telling you the plot would completely spoil the experience. This is why we refer to a newspaper article or blog with that kind of information as a spoiler.' According to the text, a 'spoiler' is ________.
choices: a review that only praises a book or film without any criticism
a newspaper article or blog that reveals information about a plot
a type of fiction that intentionally has no clear ending
a reader who dislikes reading about unexpected events

question: Read the following excerpt: 'In a study, participants were divided into two groups and each group was given the same stories to read. In addition, the second group were also given brief introductions which subtly spoilt the plots. You might assume that the first group found the stories more engaging, but surprisingly, it was the second group who claimed the greatest reading satisfaction.' What was the surprising result of this study?
choices: Both groups found the stories equally enjoyable to read
The group without spoilers reported the highest satisfaction
The group that received spoilers reported the greatest reading satisfaction
Neither group was able to finish reading the stories provided

question: Read the following excerpt: 'The research carried out showed that the kind of people who are keen to avoid spoilers by accident tend to have a particular personality type. They are very emotionally involved with what they are reading.' According to this text, people who avoid spoilers tend to be ________.
choices: less intelligent than people who enjoy spoilers
not interested in the characters or the plot
very careful about what they read in general
very emotionally involved with what they are reading

question: According to information about Jordan's government, the Prime Minister is ________ by the King.
choices: appointed
elected
nominated by the senate
chosen by public vote

question: In Jordan's National Assembly, the Senate (Majlis Al-Aayan) serves as the ________ house.
choices: lower
upper
elected
temporary

question: According to the article on resilience, 'resilience is something that people build by ________.'
choices: avoiding all stressful and challenging situations in life
relying entirely on others for support and guidance
going through difficulties and coming out the other side
ignoring problems and focusing only on positive emotions

question: The poem 'Stopping by Woods on a Snowy Evening' was written by ________ and published in 1923.
choices: William Shakespeare
T.S. Eliot
Emily Dickinson
Robert Frost
